title: AI Solutions Engineer
company_focus:
Subscription economy platform for recurring billing and
monetization
description:
About Zuora

At Zuora, we do Modern Business. We’re helping people
subscribe to new ways of doing business that are better for
people, companies, and, ultimately, the planet. It’s an
approach resulting from the shift to the Subscription
Economy that puts customers first by building recurring
relationships instead of one-time product sales and focuses
on sustainable growth. Through our leading expertise and
multi-product suite, we are transforming all industries and
working with the world’s most innovative companies to
monetize new business models, nurture subscriber
relationships, and optimize their digital experiences.

The Opportunity and About the Team Our Corporate Operation
team is Zuora's internal technology organization,
responsible for creating technology experiences that connect
our teams, drive business alignment and build a stronger,
more collaborative workplace.” With a cloud-first approach,
we empower our global ZEOs with increased productivity and
self-service to enable company growth, scale, and
flexibility while hardening our security and compliance
posture. The Digital Workplace team is responsible for all
of our technology systems providing solutions for Zuora
employees including internal Accounting, Procurement, Legal,
and Human Resources departments.

As an AI Solutions Engineer on the team, you'll have the
opportunity to:

- Deploy AI-Powered Tools: Roll out and fine-tune AI solutions
  that embed intelligence into everyday workflows (e.g.
  configuring enterprise search assistant and AI service
  agents) to improve how employees find information and
  resolve requests.  You will provide end-to-end
  implementation for emerging solutions to full deployment
  and adoption.
- Build & Optimize AI Agents: Use  agent builder  frameworks
  to create custom AI agents that help various internal
  teams. This includes designing, testing, and refining
  prompts to ensure our AI assistants (chatbots, search
  agents, etc.) provide accurate, context-aware responses.
  You’ll apply prompt engineering techniques to continuously
  improve agent performance and reduce errors.
- Improve Internal Workflows: Collaborate with cross-
  functional teams to identify high-impact opportunities for
  AI automation.. You’ll work with stakeholders in Corporate
  Operations, HR, Finance and customer facing teams to map
  out processes that can be enhanced by AI (from workflow
  automation to decision support), then prototype and
  implement solutions that address those needs.
- Integrate Systems & Data: Connect AI platforms with our
  internal tools and data sources. You’ll manage
  configurations, API integrations, and data pipelines to
  ensure the AI solutions have the information they need..
  For example, you might integrate our knowledge bases or
  ticketing data so that the AI agents can access up-to-date
  company information when answering questions.
- Enable & Support Users: Serve as an internal champion for
  these AI tools. You will create clear documentation and
  conduct training sessions and demos to help employees get
  the most out of new AI features.  Part of your job is to
  gather user feedback and monitor usage metrics, using
  those insights to iterate on prompts/configurations for
  continuous improvement. You’ll ensure a smooth adoption
  and drive ongoing enhancements so that the AI solutions
  continue to deliver value.

About You

The business outcomes you will impact include (but are not
limited to):

- Improvement of Current Systems:
- New Product Feature Development:
- Best Practices and Governance:

Your experience and skills

- Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science,
  Information Systems, Machine Learning or a related field.
  (Candidates with Minimum 2 years of experience are welcome
  – we care most about your skills and eagerness to learn.)
- AI/ML Knowledge: Foundational understanding of AI and
  machine learning concepts – especially familiarity with
  large language models (LLMs) and how they can be applied
  to solve real-world problems. You stay curious and keep up
  with the latest in AI.
- Hands-on with AI Tools: Demonstrated interest or experience
  in working with AI-powered platforms or APIs (e.g.
  building a small chatbot, using an AI knowledge search, or
  automating workflows with AI). Proven ability to quickly
  learn new AI tools and make them useful. Experience with
  enterprise AI solutions (such as Glean, OpenAI,
  Atomicwork, Gemini, Slack, etc.) is a plus.
- Scripting & Integration Skills: Comfortable with light
  coding/scripting (Python, JavaScript or similar) to
  customize solutions. Ability to work with APIs and
  webhooks to integrate systems is important.   You don’t
  need to be a full-time software engineer, but you should
  be able to tweak code or configurations to connect tools
  together.
- Problem-Solving Mindset: Strong analytical and problem-
  solving skills. You can break down a business workflow,
  identify where it can be improved, and configure an AI
  solution to do it. When an AI response isn’t quite right,
  you iterate on the prompt or data until it improves.
- Communication & Collaboration: Excellent communication
  skills and a collaborative approach to working with non-
  technical stakeholders. You can translate technical AI
  capabilities into plain language, helping teams understand
  and trust the solutions. You thrive in a cross-functional
  setting, working with IT colleagues, business users, and
  vendors alike.

Pay and Benefits

#ZEOLife at Zuora

As an industry pioneer, our work constantly evolves and
challenges us in new ways that require us to think
differently, iterate often, and learn continually—it’s
exciting. Our people, whom we refer to as “ZEOs" are
empowered to take on a mindset of ownership and make a
bigger impact here. Our teams collaborate deeply and
exchange different ideas openly. Together, we’re making
what’s next possible for our customers, community, and the
world.

As part of our commitment to building an inclusive, high-
performance culture where ZEOs feel inspired, connected, and
valued, we support ZEOs with:

- Competitive compensation, corporate bonus programs,
  performance rewards, company equity, and retirement
  programs
- Medical insurance
- Generous, flexible time off
- Paid holidays, “wellness” days, and company-wide end-of-year
  break
- Paid parental leave
- Learning & Development stipend
- Opportunities to volunteer and give back, including
  charitable donation match
- Free resources and support for your mental wellbeing

Specific benefits and offerings can be discussed during the
interview process.

Location & Work Arrangements

Organizations and teams at Zuora are empowered to design
efficient and flexible working methods, being intentional
about scheduling, communication, and collaboration
strategies that help us achieve our best results. In our
dynamic, globally distributed company, this means balancing
flexibility and responsibility — flexibility to live our
lives to the fullest and responsibility to each other, our
customers, and our shareholders. This location-specific
position requires you to come to the Chennai  Zuora office
at least thrice a week on average!

Our Commitment to an Inclusive Workplace

Think, be, and do you! At Zuora, different perspectives,
experiences, and contributions matter. Everyone counts.
Zuora is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer committed
to creating an inclusive environment for all.

Zuora does not discriminate based on and considers
individuals seeking employment with Zuora without regard to
race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including
pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or
related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender
identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected
veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic
information, political views or activity, or other
applicable legally protected characteristics.

We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.
Applicants requiring special assistance or accommodation
during the interview process or while accessing our website
may email us at assistance(at)zuora.com.
responsibilities: Deploy and fine-tune AI-powered tools including enterprise search assistants and AI service agents for internal workflows
Build and optimize custom AI agents using agent builder frameworks, including prompt design, testing, and refinement
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify AI automation opportunities and prototype solutions
Integrate AI platforms with internal tools and data sources through API integrations and data pipelines
Create documentation, conduct training sessions, and serve as an internal champion for AI tools
Gather user feedback and monitor usage metrics to iterate on prompts and configurations for continuous improvement
use_cases: Enterprise search assistant for employee information discovery
AI service agents for internal request resolution
AI chatbots for HR, Finance, and Corporate Operations support
Workflow automation and decision support for internal teams
Knowledge base integration for contextual AI responses
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves hands-on work deploying and fine-tuning
AI solutions, building custom AI agents using agent builder
frameworks, and applying prompt engineering techniques to
improve LLM performance. The engineer works directly on
implementing and configuring AI assistants (chatbots, search
agents) with end-to-end ownership from prototyping to
deployment.
